assertion_results.text: Data extraction correctly identifies CVE-2026-31812, library quinn-proto, affected range < 0.11.14, and fixed version 0.11.14 from the Vulnerability issue (Step 1)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md lines 7-13 contain: CVE ID = CVE-2026-31812, Vulnerable library = quinn-proto, Affected version range = 'versions before 0.11.14', Fixed version = 0.11.14. All four data points are correctly extracted.

assertion_results.text: Data extraction identifies the ecosystem as Cargo based on the library name and component context (Step 1 Ecosystem detection)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md line 20 shows 'Ecosystem | Cargo'. The Ecosystem Detection section (lines 29-35) explains: 'Library quinn-proto is a Rust crate' and 'Ecosystem Mappings in both streams list Cargo with lock file Cargo.lock'.

assertion_results.text: Version impact table includes ALL versions from the security-matrix.md supportability matrix — no versions are skipped (Important Rule 4)
assertion_results.evidence: The security-matrix-mock.md defines 7 versions across two streams: 2.1.0, 2.1.1 (2.1.x stream) and 2.2.0, 2.2.1, 2.2.2, 2.2.3, 2.2.4 (2.2.x stream). version-impact.md lines 9-15 include all 7 versions in its table: 2.1.0, 2.1.1, 2.2.0, 2.2.1, 2.2.2, 2.2.3, 2.2.4. No versions are skipped.

assertion_results.text: Version impact analysis uses pinned commit tags from the supportability matrix for each version, not branch HEAD (Important Rule 13)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md Pinned Tag column shows: v0.3.8 (2.1.0), v0.3.12 (2.1.1), v0.4.5 (2.2.0), v0.4.8 (2.2.1), v0.4.9 (2.2.2), v0.4.11 (2.2.3), v0.4.12 (2.2.4). These correspond to the supportability matrix tags. For 2.2.2, the output uses v0.4.9 (the build tag) rather than v0.4.8 (the backend column tag), but the analysis correctly identifies it as a retag and uses pinned commit data rather than branch HEAD. The Evidence section (line 20) explicitly states: 'Lock file method: git show <tag>:Cargo.lock per Ecosystem Mappings'.

assertion_results.text: Version impact table identifies retag versions and carries forward the affected status from the original version (Important Rule 5)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md line 13 for version 2.2.2 shows: 'retag of 2.2.1 (same as 2.2.1)' with Affected = YES and quinn-proto = 0.11.12. The Evidence section (line 21) states: 'Retag handling: Version 2.2.2 uses tag v0.4.9 which is a retag of v0.4.8 (used by 2.2.1). Lock file check skipped; result carried forward from 2.2.1 (quinn-proto 0.11.12, AFFECTED).'

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction proposes replacing PSIRT-assigned versions with lock-file-verified versions, showing the diff (Step 3.2)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md lines 12-14 show the diff: 'Current (PSIRT-assigned) | RHTPA 2.0.0' vs 'Proposed (lock file evidence) | RHTPA 2.2.0, RHTPA 2.2.1, RHTPA 2.2.2'. The Proposed Comment section (lines 43-53) shows the correction as '[RHTPA 2.0.0] -> [RHTPA 2.2.0, RHTPA 2.2.1, RHTPA 2.2.2]' with per-version lock file evidence.

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction references version names from the supportability matrix rather than hardcoded Jira version IDs (Important Rule 6)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md lines 30-36 show the proposed Jira mutation using version names: '{"name": "RHTPA 2.2.0"}', '{"name": "RHTPA 2.2.1"}', '{"name": "RHTPA 2.2.2"}'. Line 39 explicitly states: 'Version IDs would be discovered dynamically via getJiraIssueTypeMetaWithFields at runtime. Version names from the supportability matrix are used here; actual Jira version IDs are resolved at execution time.' No hardcoded Jira version IDs are present.

assertion_results.text: Remediation tasks include Repository, Target Branch, acceptance criteria, and dependency linkage between upstream and downstream tasks (Important Rule 9)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Task 1 (2.2.x upstream backport): Repository = 'backend' (line 22), Target Branch = 'release/0.4.z' (line 26), Acceptance Criteria with checklist (lines 49-51), dependency link to TC-8001 (line 61). Task 2 (2.2.x downstream propagation): Repository = 'rhtpa-release.0.4.z' (line 73), Target Branch = 'main' (line 79), Acceptance Criteria (lines 98-99), dependency linkage (lines 107-108). Blocks linkage between upstream and downstream shown at lines 119-121: 'jira.create_link(inwardIssue: <upstream-task-key>, outwardIssue: <downstream-task-key>, type: "Blocks")'.

assertion_results.text: For Cargo ecosystem, remediation creates two tasks: upstream backport task and downstream propagation subtask with Blocks dependency (Important Rule 8)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md creates two tasks for the 2.2.x stream: Task 1 'Upstream Backport' (line 12, summary: 'Remediate CVE-2026-31812: bump quinn-proto to 0.11.14 (rhtpa-2.2)') and Task 2 'Downstream Propagation' (line 64, summary: 'Propagate CVE-2026-31812 fix: update backend ref in rhtpa-release.0.4.z (rhtpa-2.2)'). Lines 119-121 create the Blocks dependency: 'jira.create_link(inwardIssue: <upstream-task-key>, outwardIssue: <downstream-task-key>, type: "Blocks")'. The same two-task pattern is also applied for the 2.1.x preemptive stream (Tasks 3 and 4, with Blocks link at line 255).

assertion_results.text: The analysis presents triage recommendations as proposed actions — framing Affects Versions changes, label additions, and status transitions as proposals rather than executed mutations (Guardrails)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Post-Triage Summary (line 274) is titled 'Proposed Actions' and frames all actions as proposals: '1. Add label ai-cve-triaged to TC-8001', '2. Correct Affects Versions', '3. Create 2 remediation tasks', etc. affects-versions.md uses 'Proposed' language throughout: 'Proposed (lock file evidence)' (line 14), 'Proposed Jira Mutation' (line 29), 'Proposed Comment' (line 42). The Jira mutation code blocks in affects-versions.md and remediation.md are presented as specifications of what to do, not as executed commands. No output claims mutations were already performed.
